metric_value: ~70% classroom; ~25% TBT

metric_name: Higher education online course offering rate

metric_name: Higher education online learning penetration forecast

metric_name: Travel cost share of corporate training budget

metric_name: e-Learning benefit: Elimination of travel costs
metric_value: Virtual learning delivery eliminates travel expenses and leaves students in place
notes: From e-Learning vs Classroom Training comparison table

metric_value: e-Learning enables personalized curricula vs generalized classroom training

metric_name: e-Learning benefit: Rapid deployment and continuous evolution
metric_value: e-Learning enables rapid deployment and continuous course updates vs slow classroom refresh
notes: Addresses fast-changing environment challenge

metric_value: Modular e-learning provides only necessary information in smaller blocks vs just-in-case classroom
notes: More complex jobs require targeted modular training; key instructional design shift

metric_value: Emerging; enabling technology for self-paced enterprise learning; clear advantages over classroom

metric_value: Emerging; instructor-led collaborative virtual classroom; addresses interaction gap
notes: Synchronous WBT enables real-time interaction; higher infrastructure requirements than async

metric_value: Emerging infrastructure layer for enterprise e-learning administration and tracking
notes: LMS identified as critical enterprise infrastructure for e-learning deployment

metric_value: Mature but declining; being superseded by web-based delivery

metric_value: Leading e-learning content and courseware provider with library-based subscription model

metric_value: Dominant LMS and authoring platform provider (formerly Asymetrix)

metric_value: IBM Lotus providing enterprise LMS integrated with Lotus Notes groupware platform

metric_value: Dominant e-learning authoring tool vendor (Authorware; Flash; Director)

metric_name: Arthur Andersen Virtual Learning Network position
metric_value: Major professional services firm deploying internal e-learning at enterprise scale
notes: Integrated learning approach combining VLN with classroom; AA collapsed 2002 in Enron scandal

metric_value: SmartForce merged with SkillSoft in September 2002; combined company became leading enterprise e-learning platform

metric_value: click2learn merged with Docent to form SumTotal Systems in March 2004
notes: LMS market consolidation as predicted; SumTotal became leading standalone LMS vendor

metric_value: Arthur Andersen collapsed in 2002 following Enron scandal; firm dissolved August 2002
notes: AA's e-learning infrastructure dissolved with the firm; Accenture (spun off 2001) carried forward digital learning practices

metric_value: e-Learning market grew far beyond $10B forecast; global corporate e-learning market exceeded $200B by 2019
notes: Aberdeen massively underestimated the long-run growth; the $10B by 2002 target was roughly on track

metric_value: Enterprises' competitive economic advantage is related to education and knowledge
notes: Kastner's opening thesis linking continuous learning to enterprise competitiveness

metric_value: Businesses running on Internet time require training delivery that matches speed of change
notes: Kastner's qualitative argument for urgency of e-learning adoption

metric_value: COVID-19 pandemic (2020) caused overnight shift of all enterprise training to virtual/online; e-learning became mandatory
